elements.section-2411-009: Weakly nonlinear analysis at the boundary (neutral curve) of $\Omega_1$
elements.sent-2411-435: We seek a solution of (\ref{bif_eqn ) close to the boundary (neutral curve) of the first tongue, $\Omega_1$, which, for the kernel (\ref{bif_kernel ) is given by \begin{equation}D = D_0(\lambda,\overline{\epsilon}) = - \frac{\lambda^3}{4 \pi^3}\left(1-\frac{\overline{\epsilon}}{1-\lambda^2}\right) \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{\lambda}\right).\label{eqn_neutral}\end{equation} Note that, since $\lambda<1$, this means that the size of the first tongue, and indeed all the tongues, decreases as $\overline{\epsilon}$ increases, and the small epsilon tongue structure is preserved for all $0\leq \overline{\epsilon < \frac{3 {4}$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-436: In the first tongue, the longest possible wavelength is $\lambda_{\rm max (\overline{\epsilon )= \sqrt{1-\overline{\epsilon }$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-437: In this weakly nonlinear analysis, we will assume that $0 \leq \overline{\epsilon < \frac{3 {4}$, but not that $\overline{\epsilon}$ is necessarily small.}}
elements.sent-2411-438: The weakly nonlinear analysis proceeds in the usual manner \cite{KBO , by writing $D = D_0(\lambda,\overline{\epsilon ) + \delta^2 D_2$, with $\delta \ll 1$, and expanding $u = 1 + \delta u_0 + \delta^2 u_1 + \delta^3 u_2 + O(\delta^4)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-439: At $O(\delta)$ we recover the neutral curve and seek a solution of the form \begin{equation}u_0 = A_0 \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}\right).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-440: At $O(\delta^2)$, there are no secular terms on the right hand side of the equation, and a constant term and a term $\cos \left(4 \pi x/\lambda\right)$ appear in $u_1$.
elements.sent-2411-441: At $O(\delta^3)$, a secular term appears on the right hand side of the equation, and suppressing this leads to the condition \begin{equation}D_2 = -\frac{1}{4} D_0 A_0^2 f(k_1,k_2),\end{equation} where \begin{equation}f(k_1,k_2) = \frac{k_1+k_2}{k_2-4k_1} + 2k_1+2 >0,~~k_n = \frac{\lambda}{n\pi}\left(1-\frac{n^2\overline{\epsilon}}{n^2-\lambda^2}\right) \sin\left(\frac{n\pi}{\lambda}\right).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-442: Rearranging and eliminating $\delta$ gives the weakly nonlinear periodic steady state, of principal wavelength $\lambda$, as \begin{equation}u \sim 1 + 2 \sqrt{\frac{D_0-D}{D_0 f(k_1,k_2)}}\cos \left(\frac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}\right)\label{WNL}\end{equation} for $D_0-D \ll 1$, and confirms that the bifurcation is supercritical ($D < D_0$ is required for solutions other than $u=0$ and $u=1$ to exist locally.).
elements.sent-2411-443: Numerical solutions of (\ref{bif_eqn ) close to the boundary of $\Omega_1$ are in excellent agreement with (\ref{WNL ).. \end{appendix.}}}
